title: District Cleanups
description: We clean up the streets of DC to make it a better place to live.  Check their calendar for upcoming events.

title: The Ballston Runaways
description: A running group in Ballston.  Meets in front of the Compass Coffee in Ballston.  Check the intagram for more details

title: DC City Girls
description: DC’s free social club for women.  They have some coed events as well.  Check the website for more details

title: Clarendon Run Club
description: A run club in clarendon.  Meets in front of Tatte (2805 Clarendon Blvd)

title: North East Track Club
description: At NETC, new, amateur, veteran, and elite runners share a space, cross the same paths, and celebrate each others wins. We push and pull in different directions to make meaningful change through community, camaraderie, and service to our city.

schedules.time: Start time is 8 am during the summer months and 9 or 10 am during other parts of the year

title: District Running Collective
description: District Running Collective We proudly represent Washington, DC through each mile Weekly runs - Wednesdays at 6:30PM, Thursdays at 6:30AM, Saturdays at 8:30 AM

title: Dudes In The District
description: Dudes In The District is a group of men who meet up for social events in DC.  Check the website for more details

title: Washington Area Frisbee Club
description: The Washington Area Frisbee Club is a non-profit organization that promotes the sport of ultimate frisbee in the Washington, DC area.  They have a variety of leagues and events for all ages and skill levels.

title: Labrynth Board Game Night
description: A group of people who meet up to play board games.  Check the website for more details Thursday is open gaming night here at Labyrinth! Registration is not required but seating is limited. To make the most of our available seating this event will be on a first come, first served basis.

title: Washington Area Roadskaters
description: The Washington Area Roadskaters (WAR) is a non-profit organization whose mission is to promote safe and enjoyable skating in the Washington, D.C. area. WAR was founded in 1987 as Washington Area Rollerskaters and is one of the oldest skate clubs in the US.  Thousands of individuals have joined together to safely enjoy inline and roller skating in the streets of our fine city.

title: NOMA Run Club
venue: Check website for location.  Each day meets at a different location
description: NOMA Run Club is a social running club that meets every Monday and Thursday at 6:30 PM and Saturday at 8:00 AM. They offer a variety of runs, from easy to challenging, and more! (check the wesbite for more details)

title: Volo Sports
description: Volo Sports is a social sports club that offers a wide range of sports activities for adults. They offer basketball, volleyball, and soccer and more! (check the wesbite for more details)  They also offer a variety of social events, such as happy hours and game nights.

title: DC Fray
description: DC Fray is a social sports club that offers a wide range of sports activities for adults. They offer basketball, volleyball, and soccer and more! (check the wesbite for more details)  They also offer a variety of social events, such as happy hours and game nights.

title: Politics and Prose Book Clubs
description: Check the website for more information. Politics and Prose is a book club that meets at the Politics and Prose bookstore in Washington, D.C. The club is open to all and is free to join. The club meets on the first Tuesday of the month at 7:00 PM.

title: Time Left DC
description: Time Left is a way to meet new people in DC.  Book your seat now and meet 5 strangers over dinner, all matched by our personality algorithm.

title: DC Singles Meetup
description: A meetup group for singles in DC.  Check the website for more details

title: DC Swoon Speed Dating
description: Cityswoon DC: it's speed dating's cooler, more adventurous cousin.  Check the website for more details

title: Shuffle Dating
description: Speed dating but done through an app.  Check the website for more details

title: Pitch A Friend
description: Pitch A Friend is a social event that allows you to meet new people in a fun and interactive way.  Pitching your friend power-point stytle.  The event is free to attend and $5 to sign up to pitch your friend.  Check the website for more details

title: DC Road Runners
description: Running the nation's capital since 1961. DC Road Runners welcomes runners of all ability levels with a year-round schedule of events, weekly training runs, training programs, and 19 free races each year.

title: DC Front Runners
description: An LGBTQ+ running and walking club that holds a regular schedule of non-competitive group runs along Rock Creek Park, the National Mall, and the Potomac River banks. Choose your distance and set your pace. All are welcome.

title: DC Capital Striders
description: A community of runners in Washington, D.C., Northern Virginia, and Maryland that believes in running in a supportive, non-competitive environment. Great for beginners and experienced runners alike.

title: Capital Area Runners
description: A DC metropolitan area running club founded to provide group training opportunities for highly motivated and competitive minded runners of all ability levels.

title: Capital Hiking Club
description: A non-profit hiking group in the DC/MD/VA area operating since 1937. They hike 52 weeks a year with options that challenge all skill levels. One of the oldest and most active hiking clubs in the region.

schedules.location: Various trailheads in DC, MD, VA
Various trailheads in DC, MD, VA

title: Wanderbirds Hiking Club
description: Founded in 1934, the Wanderbirds is a non-profit, all-volunteer club that organizes day hikes every Sunday, year-round, for energetic folks in the DC area. Hikers range in age from 7 to over 80, and the club welcomes fast, experienced hikers as well as slower or beginning hikers.

schedules.location: Various trailheads in DC, MD, VA

title: Potomac Pedalers Touring Club
description: One of the largest bicycle clubs in the US. A non-profit educational, recreational and social organization for bicycling enthusiasts in the DC metro area with rides for all skill levels.

title: DC Paceline
description: One of Greater Washington Area's most well-known cycling training series. Four groups (A, B, C, and Juniors) ride criterium-style on a non-technical course. Great for community cycling rides in DC, Maryland, and Virginia.

title: Sends with Friends
description: A minority-led rock climbing community in the DMV area that brings together climbers of all cultural backgrounds and nationalities since 2021. Meets monthly on the first Friday at DC Bouldering Project.

schedules.location: DC Bouldering Project, 1200 Half St SE, Washington, DC 20003

title: Adventuring LGBTQ+
description: An outdoors club for the LGBTQ+ community in the Washington DC metro area. LGBTQ+ volunteers lead hikes, kayaking trips, camping, and other outdoor activities year-round. All experience levels welcome.

title: Baila DC
description: A premier salsa dance school in the DC metro area offering On2 salsa classes at 5 levels. Hosts two monthly 'Baila Thursdays' socials at La Cosecha in Union Market. No partner needed.

title: Salsa With Silvia
description: Fun and affordable Salsa, Bachata, and Reggaeton group classes in Washington, D.C. and Bethesda. Open to all levels from beginner to advanced with a welcoming social dance community.

title: DC Street Photography Collective
description: A non-profit 501(c)(3) dedicated to street photographers from the DC, MD, VA areas. Devoted to producing, sharing, and creatively capturing the human condition through photography walks and exhibits.

title: Exposed DC
description: Cultivates the Washington DC area photography community through news, highlighted images, regular social meetups, teach-ins, and an annual photography contest and exhibit. Open to photographers of all levels.

title: Solid State Books Book Clubs
description: DC's independent bookstore at Union Market hosts multiple book clubs including a Horror book club (3rd Wednesday monthly) and a Science-Fiction & Fantasy book club. A welcoming community for readers of all genres.

schedules.location: Solid State Books, 600 H St NE, Washington, DC 20002

title: ZogSports DC
description: Sign up as a whole team or register individually for coed recreational sports leagues including volleyball, kickball, dodgeball, softball, and more. Leagues end with happy hours and social events.

title: OutLoud Sports DC
description: A leading LGBTQ+ recreational sports organization in Washington DC that promotes inclusivity and fun through diverse sports including sand volleyball, tennis, pickleball, and dodgeball. All are welcome regardless of identity.

title: DC Pickleball League
description: Multi-week ladder leagues with both social and competitive divisions at Rock Creek and WTEF. Great for meeting fellow pickleball players in the DC area at all skill levels.

title: Free Yoga For Everyone DC
description: A Meetup group with over 1,000 members that practices traditional Ashtanga yoga together completely free of charge in open areas and parks in DC and Northern Virginia. All levels welcome, no experience needed.

title: Spanglish Exchange DC
description: A language exchange that brings together native English speakers and native Spanish speakers in a relaxed social setting. Practice conversation skills while making new friends over drinks.

title: DC Language and Cultural Exchange Club
description: A social group where people get together to practice and learn new languages with weekly language exchange meetings. Mainly a social for intermediate, advanced, and native speakers of many languages.

title: Washington Area Bicyclist Association
description: WABA advocates for better biking in the DC region and organizes community rides, bike-to-work events, and cycling education classes. They also lead group rides and social cycling events throughout the year.

title: Take a Hike DMV
description: A friendly and inclusive group for anyone who loves exploring the outdoors in the DC, Maryland, and Virginia area. They organize hikes across the DMV focusing on scenic and accessible trails within an easy drive of DC.

title: Pour House Trivia
description: One of the largest trivia companies in the DC region with over 800 teams participating each week at dozens of bars and restaurants. Free to play with gift card prizes. Check their website for the full venue schedule.

title: Soul Trak Outdoors
description: A DC-based nonprofit organization that connects communities of color to outdoor spaces through hiking, climbing, and nature experiences. Meets monthly on the third Sunday at DC Bouldering Project.

schedules.location: DC Bouldering Project, 1200 Half St SE, Washington, DC 20003
